title: Siparuna neglecta
intro: Siparuna neglecta, also known as the "Guianese tree", is a species of plant in the family Siparunaceae, native to the Guiana Shield region. The health benefits of Siparuna neglecta include its potential anti-inflammatory and antiparasitic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Siparuna neglecta include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Siparuna neglecta is commonly used to prepare decoctions and infusions, which are often used to treat fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
